title: Torus Object
description: Transient torus. A transient torus is not displayed or saved in a document. A transient torus is used as a wrapper to work with raw torus information. A transient torus is a full torus with no boundaries. They are created statically using the create method of the Torus class.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: copy
methods.description: Creates and returns an independent copy of this Torus object.

methods.name: getData
methods.description: Gets all of the data defining the torus.

methods.name: set
methods.description: Sets all of the data defining the torus.

methods.name: transformBy
methods.description: Updates this surface by transforming it with a given input matrix.

properties.name: axis
properties.description: Gets and sets the center axis of the torus.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: majorRadius
properties.description: Gets and sets the major radius of the torus.

properties.name: minorRadius
properties.description: Gets and sets the minor radius of the torus.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: origin
properties.description: Gets and sets the origin point (center) of the torus.

axis.title: Torus.axis Property
axis.description: Gets and sets the center axis of the torus.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = torus_var.axis

# Set the value of the property.
torus_var.axis = propertyValue

# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = torus_var.evaluator

isValid.title: Torus.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = torus_var.isValid

majorRadius.description: Gets and sets the major radius of the torus.

syntax.python:

propertyValue = torus_var.majorRadius

# Set the value of the property.
torus_var.majorRadius = propertyValue

minorRadius.title: Torus.minorRadius Property
minorRadius.description: Gets and sets the minor radius of the torus.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = torus_var.minorRadius

# Set the value of the property.
torus_var.minorRadius = propertyValue

objectType.title: Torus.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = torus_var.objectType

origin.title: Torus.origin Property
origin.description: Gets and sets the origin point (center) of the torus.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = torus_var.origin

# Set the value of the property.
torus_var.origin = propertyValue

# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = torus_var.surfaceType

classType.title: Torus.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

copy.title: Torus.copy Method
copy.description: Creates and returns an independent copy of this Torus object.
copy.return_description: Returns a new Torus object that is a copy of this Torus object.

create.title: Torus.create Method
create.return_description: Returns the new Torus object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: origin
parameters.description: The origin point (center) of the torus.

parameters.name: axis
parameters.description: The center axis of the torus.

parameters.name: majorRadius
parameters.description: The major radius of the torus.

parameters.name: minorRadius
parameters.description: The minor radius of the torus.

getData.title: Torus.getData Method
getData.description: Gets all of the data defining the torus.

parameters.name: origin
parameters.description: The output origin point (center) of the torus.

parameters.name: axis
parameters.description: The output center axis of the torus.

parameters.name: majorRadius
parameters.description: The output major radius of the torus.

parameters.name: minorRadius
parameters.description: The output minor radius of the torus.

set.title: Torus.set Method
set.description: Sets all of the data defining the torus.

parameters.name: origin
parameters.description: The origin point (center) of the torus.

parameters.name: axis
parameters.description: The center axis of the torus.

parameters.name: majorRadius
parameters.description: The major radius of the torus.

parameters.name: minorRadius
parameters.description: The minor radius of the torus.

transformBy.title: Torus.transformBy Method
transformBy.description: Updates this surface by transforming it with a given input matrix.
transformBy.return_description: Returns true if the transform was successful.

parameters.name: matrix
parameters.description: A 3D matrix that defines the transform to apply to the surface.
